questions.question: Which of the following digital logic gates is considered 'universal' because any other Boolean function can be implemented using only this type of gate?
In a virtual memory system, what is a 'page fault'?
options: An error that occurs when the CPU tries to access data from an invalid memory address.
An event where the CPU attempts to access a page that is currently not present in physical memory.
A hardware malfunction indicating a corrupted page table entry.
A condition where two different virtual addresses map to the same physical address.
The successful retrieval of data from a page in cache memory.

questions.question: Which power optimization technique in digital IC design involves selectively disabling the clock signal to inactive functional blocks to reduce dynamic power consumption?

In formal verification of digital circuits, which technique systematically explores all possible states of a finite-state model of a system to prove or disprove properties expressed in temporal logic (e.g., LTL or CTL)?
What is the primary purpose of 'register renaming' in modern out-of-order execution processors?
options: To reduce the number of physical registers required by the CPU.
To eliminate false data dependencies (Write-After-Read (WAR) and Write-After-Write (WAW) hazards) and enable out-of-order execution.
To allow multiple instructions to simultaneously write to the same architectural register.
To convert architectural registers into memory-mapped registers.
To resolve control hazards by predicting branch outcomes.
